subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multidigit- numerals up to 1,000,000
indicator_description: Model number quantities up to 1,000,000 using graph sheets and multi-base block
examplars:
1. Ask pupils to model number quantities up to 1,000,000 using graph sheets or multi-base materials.
2. Ask pupils to model the number 137,000 shading graph sheet square.
3. Give learners teacher-make token currency notes [¢10, ¢100 & ¢500 notes] to model amounts up to ¢10,000.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multidigit- numerals up to 1,000,000
indicator_description: Read and write numbers in figures and in words up to 1,000,000
examplars:
1. Play the place value number wheel game to generate 7-digit numbers.
2. Ask pupils to write given numbers on the expanded form.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multidigit- numerals up to 1,000,000
indicator_description: Identify numbers in different positions around a given number in a number chart
examplars: 1. Display a number chart in multiples of 1,500 between 10,000 and 1,000,000 and identify positions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multidigit- numerals up to 1,000,000
indicator_description: Compare and order whole numbers up to 100,000 and represent the comparison using '>, <, or ='
examplars:
1. Identify numbers which are 10,000 more or less than a given six-digit number.
2. Order a given set of numbers in ascending or descending order.
3. Encourage learners to say as many things as possible about two given numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multidigit- numerals up to 1,000,000
indicator_description: Round (off, up, down) whole numbers up to 100,000 to the nearest ten thousands, thousands, hundreds and tens
examplars:
1. Round off numbers to the nearest 10,000, 1,000 and 100.
2. Round up and round down to estimate sums and differences.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multidigit- numerals up to 1,000,000
indicator_description: Skip count forwards and backwards in 500s 1000s etc. up to and from 100,000
examplars:
1. Group activity to skip count in 500s or 1,000s.
2. Correct errors or identify omissions in skip count sequences.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.2 Demonstrate understanding of Roman Numerals up to C (i.e. 100)
indicator_description: Recognize Roman Numerals system up to C (i.e. 100)
examplars:
1. Display charts and identify main characters I, V, X, L, and C.
2. Match Roman numerals to Hindu-Arabic numerals.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.2 Demonstrate understanding of Roman Numerals up to C (i.e. 100)
indicator_description: Count and convert Hindu Arabic numbers to Roman numerals up to 100 (C) and vice versa
examplars:
1. Read the chart in various directions.
2. Convert numbers between Hindu Arabic and Roman systems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of factors, multiples of numbers including composite, even, odd and prime numbers from 1 to 100
indicator_description: Identify the factors of whole numbers 1-100
examplars:
1. Use different arrangements of arrays of objects to find factors.
2. Collect from arrays to form pairs of factors.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of factors, multiples of numbers including composite, even, odd and prime numbers from 1 to 100
indicator_description: Generate and identify prime numbers and composite numbers between 1 and 100
examplars:
1. Use arrays to find factors and identify prime vs composite numbers.
2. Use the 'Sieve of Eratosthenes' to identify prime numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of quantities and place value for multidigit- numerals up to 100,000
indicator_description: Identify even and odd numbers between 1 and 100 as number that can be arrayed in twos array and those which cannot
examplars:
1. Use twos arrays of objects to find even and odd numbers.
2. Skip count in twos starting from 1 for odd numbers and from 2 for even numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of factors, multiples of numbers including composite, even, odd and prime numbers from 1 to 100
indicator_description: Determine the highest common factor of any 2 or 3 numbers by prime factorisation
examplars:
1. Use factor trees to determine prime factors.
2. Underline common factors to determine HCF.
3. Use prime factorization to determine LCM.
4. Place factors in Venn diagrams to find HCF and LCM.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of factors, multiples of numbers including composite, even, odd and prime numbers from 1 to 100
indicator_description: Recognize relationship between factors and multiples of whole numbers from 1 to 100
examplars:
1. Investigate how a number is determined to be even or odd.
2. Investigate divisibility tests for multiples of 4 and 6.
3. Investigate perfect numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.2.1 Describe and apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties to determine answers for basic multiplication facts to 81 and related division facts
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties to determine answers for basic multiplication facts to 81 and related division facts
examplars:
1. Describe strategies like skip counting from a known fact, doubling/halving, 9s patterns, and repeated doubling.
2. Recall basic facts.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.2.1 Describe and apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties to determine answers for basic multiplication facts to 81 and related division facts
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies for multiplication, such as annexing then adding zero halving and doubling using the distributive property
examplars:
1. Annex or add zeros when multiplying by multiples of 10, 100, or 1000.
2. Apply halving and doubling for products like 32x5.
3. Apply distributive property for expanded forms or numbers close to 10.
4. Explain multiplication properties of one and zero.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of multiplication of a 2 or 3-digit number by a 2 or 3-digit number.
indicator_description: Multiply multi digit numbers by 2-digit numbers efficiently
examplars:
1. Use 'expand and box' (partial decomposition) method.
2. Use Column or vertical method.
3. Use Distributive property.
4. Use Lattice method.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.2.3 Recall basic division fact up to 100
indicator_description: Determine basic division fact up to 81
examplars:
1. Investigate divisibility tests for 3, 4, 6, 8, and 9.
2. Play the 3-in-a-line quotient game on a multiplication chart.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.2.4 Demonstrate an understanding of division of a 2 or 3-digit number by a 1 or 2-digit number
indicator_description: Divide 3-digit numbers by I-digit number efficiently
examplars: 1. Long division method explaining repeated subtraction.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.2.5 Translate word problems into mathematical sentences and solve
indicator_description: Solve multi step word problems involving the four basic operations
examplars:
1. Model statements involving addition and multiplication.
2. Model statements involving division and subtraction.
3. Role play word problems.
4. Use calculators and spreadsheets to assess reasonableness.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.2.6 Demonstrate understanding of integers
indicator_description: Solve simple addition and subtraction problems involving integers
examplars:
1. Use number line for addition problems.
2. Use number line for subtraction problems.
3. Solve word problems involving underwater depth and opposite numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting and multiplying fractions
indicator_description: Determine equivalent fractions of given fractions
examplars: 1. Use LCD to determine equivalent fractions for comparison or operations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting and multiplying fractions
indicator_description: Compare and order fractions
examplars:
1. Find LCD to express equivalent fractions for comparison.
2. Change fractions to decimals or percentages for comparison.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting and multiplying fractions
indicator_description: Use the concept of equivalent fractions in changing fractions to the same denominator for addition and subtraction of like fractions
examplars:
1. Use LCD to add/subtract fractions with denominators being multiples of others.
2. Find sums and differences using equivalent fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting and multiplying fractions
indicator_description: Use the concept of equivalent fractions for addition and subtraction of fractions greater than one (improper or mixed fractions)
examplars:
1. Add like mixed fractions by summing whole numbers and adding fractions.
2. Subtract by changing mixed to improper fractions.
3. Use LCD for addition/subtraction of improper fractions with different denominators.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting and multiplying fractions
indicator_description: Use models to explain the result of multiplying a whole number by a fraction
examplars:
1. Interpreting multiplication as repeated addition.
2. Multiply whole numbers by mixed fractions using distributive property.
3. Multiply using common fraction forms.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.3.1 Demonstrate understanding of strategies for comparing, adding, subtracting and multiplying fractions
indicator_description: Use models to explain the result of multiplying a fraction by whole number
examplars:
1. Interpret multiplication as 'of' using shaded sheets.
2. Multiply mixed fractions by whole numbers by converting to improper fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of decimals fractions and strategies for comparing, adding and subtracting decimal fraction
indicator_description: Describe and represent decimals (up to the thousandths) concretely, pictorially, and symbolically and relate them to fractions
examplars:
1. Identify fractions on number lines or pictorial representations.
2. Use place value charts to write decimal numbers.
3. Convert common/improper fractions to thousandths.
4. Use graph sheets to model decimal numbers like 0.133.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of decimals fractions and strategies for comparing, adding and subtracting decimal fraction
indicator_description: Compare and order decimal fractions and a mixture of common and decimal fractions (up to the thousandths) using the symbols <, >, or =
examplars:
1. Express mixture in one form (common, decimal, or percent) for ordering.
2. Locate fractions on number line for ordering.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of decimals fractions and strategies for comparing, adding and subtracting decimal fraction
indicator_description: Round decimals to the nearest tenth and hundredth
examplars:
1. Explain rules for rounding to tenth or hundredth place.
2. Use tables to practice rounding decimal fractions.
3. Change fractions to decimals and round result.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of decimals fractions and strategies for comparing, adding and subtracting decimal fraction
indicator_description: Use models to explain the result of addition and subtraction of decimals (up to the thousandths)
examplars:
1. Align decimal points to add hundredths and thousandths.
2. Align decimal points to subtract thousandths from tenths.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.4.1 Demonstrate understanding of decimals fractions and strategies for comparing, adding and subtracting decimal fraction
indicator_description: Use models to explain the result of multiplying a decimal (up to the thousandths) by a whole number
examplars:
1. Review multiplying fractions by whole numbers.
2. Convert decimal to common fraction for multiplication.
3. Ignore decimal point, multiply whole numbers, then place decimal point based on total places.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of percentage of a given number
indicator_description: Determine the percentage of a given quantity (limit to 2-digit whole number) and vice versa
examplars:
1. Review fraction multiplication.
2. Convert percentage to common fraction to multiply and simplify.
3. Find specific percentages of quantities (e.g. 20% of 45).

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of percentage of a given number
indicator_description: Determine the benchmark percentages from their common fractions and use these to estimate percentages of quantities
examplars:
1. Use charts to identify benchmark percentages from equivalent fractions.
2. Drill benchmark fraction equivalences through games.
3. Estimate percentages mentally using benchmarks.
4. Verify estimated percentages through formal calculation.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.1.5.1 Demonstrate understanding of percentage of a given number
indicator_description: Identify and describe percent from real-life contexts and solve problems using percent.
examplars:
1. Discuss shop discounts (50%), exam marks, and battery power.
2. Draw and interpret circle graphs representing percentages.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Extend a given pattern with and without concrete materials, and explain how each element differs from the preceding one
examplars:
1. Skip count in multiples of 20, 50, 500.
2. Use skip counting to find missing numbers on charts or lines.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Describe, orally or in writing, a given pattern, using mathematical language
examplars:
1. Tabulate results for line intersection sequences.
2. Count joints in square tile wall designs to complete tables and describe relationships.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Predict subsequent elements in a given pattern
examplars:
1. Find next terms and state pattern rules.
2. Complete tables for match stick patterns in geometric shapes.
3. Pose questions for peers to find future terms in a pattern.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Represent a given pattern visually to verify predictions
examplars:
1. Examine match stick patterns for triangles and complete tables.
2. Examine match stick patterns for squares and complete tables.
3. Pose match stick requirement questions for future pattern steps.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Solve a given problem by using a pattern rule to determine subsequent elements
examplars:
1. Describe rules and determine next three elements for numeric patterns.
2. Determine next elements for decimal or halving patterns.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Determine and explain why a given number is or is not the next element in a pattern
examplars: 1. Identify and describe errors in marble-filling patterns.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Write a rule in words and in algebra to represent a given pattern
examplars: 1. State rules in words and algebra for linear patterns (e.g. 7n, 4(n-1)).

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.1.1 Determine the pattern rule to make predictions about subsequent elements.
indicator_description: Describe the relationship in a given table or chart, using a mathematical expression
examplars:
1. Explain pattern and determine trip participants from boxed lunch costs.
2. Write questions based on numeric patterns for peers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.2.1 Demonstrate understanding of algebraic expressions
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of algebraic expressions as mathematical phrases that can contain letters which represent ordinary numbers and operators
examplars:
1. Write algebraic expressions for word problems.
2. Write algebraic expressions for the perimeter of shapes.
3. Simplify basic expressions by grouping like terms.
4. Substitute values into expressions and calculate answers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.3.1 Solve problems involving single variable, one-step equations with whole number coefficients
indicator_description: Express a given problem as an equation where the unknown is represented by a letter to variable.
examplars:
1. Solve word problems regarding costs and products.
2. Complete tables and write mathematical sentences for plant growth over days.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.3.1 Solve problems involving single variable, one-step equations with whole number coefficients
indicator_description: Identify the unknown in a problem; represent the problem with an equation; and solve the problem concretely, pictorially or symbolically.
examplars: 1. Use concrete materials or balance scales to solve for unknowns.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.2.3.1 Solve problems involving single variable, one-step equations with whole number coefficients
indicator_description: Create a problem for a given equation
examplars:
1. Describe stories that a numeric equation could represent.
2. Solve numeric puzzles by solving equations in each line.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.1.1 Identify and sort quadrilaterals, including rectangles, squares, trapezoids, parallelograms, and rhombuses, according to their attributes
indicator_description: Demonstrate an understanding of the properties (e.g. sides, angles, and diagonals) of squares and rectangles
examplars: 1. Identify squares and rectangles on worksheets and provide reasoning.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.1.1 Identify and sort quadrilaterals, including rectangles, squares, trapezoids, parallelograms, and rhombuses, according to their attributes
indicator_description: Use paper folding with cut-out squares and rectangles to investigate sides, angles, and diagonals properties
examplars: 1. Use foldable cut-outs to complete property tables for squares and rectangles.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.1.1 Identify and sort quadrilaterals, including rectangles, squares, trapezoids, parallelograms, and rhombuses, according to their attributes
indicator_description: Prove that a polygon is regular by measuring the sides and angles or by folding and superimposing
examplars: 1. Investigate and tabulate properties for various quadrilaterals using foldable cut-outs.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.2.1 Estimate and measure perimeter and surface area of 2-D shapes using centimetre and metre
indicator_description: Estimate perimeter using referents for centimetre and metre, and calculate the actual perimeter and compare.
examplars: 1. Use personal referents to estimate and then measure classroom objects.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.2.1 Estimate and measure perimeter and surface area of 2-D shapes using centimetre and metre
indicator_description: Calculate perimeter of given shapes in centimetres and metres.
examplars: 1. Find perimeter of 2D shapes by adding dimensions of all sides.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.2.1 Estimate and measure perimeter and surface area of 2-D shapes using centimetre and metre
indicator_description: Calculate surface area of given shapes in centimetres and metres.
examplars: 1. Calculate surface area of compound 2D shapes by splitting them into rectangles.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of volume of common 3D shapes
indicator_description: Select and justify referents for cm3 or m3 units.
examplars:
1. Examine objects representing 1cm3 and 1m3.
2. Find community referents for volume (e.g. sugar cube, polytank).
3. Identify classroom containers for specific volume units.
4. State the numeric relationship between 1cm3 and 1m3.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of volume of common 3D shapes
indicator_description: Determine the volume of boxes by finding how many cubes of sizes 1cm3 each contains
examplars:
1. Fill containers with unit cubes to determine volume.
2. Determine volume for multiple boxes.
3. Deduce the mathematical rule for box volume (l x w x h).

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of volume of common 3D shapes.
indicator_description: Determine different sizes of boxes that have the same volume.
examplars:
1. Use a set number of unit cubes to draw boxes with different dimensions.
2. Investigate all possible box designs for fixed cube counts.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of volume of common 3D shapes.
indicator_description: Demonstrate an understanding of capacity by describing the relationship between capacity and volume as well as litre and 10cm3
examplars: 1. Fill and empty containers to state relationships between cm3 and litres.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.3.3 Demonstrate an understanding of angles
indicator_description: Identify examples of angles in the environment that are equal to, less than or larger than, a right angle
examplars:
1. Identify environment angles like laptop/door openings.
2. Fold right angles from paper and categorize classroom angles.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.3.3 Demonstrate an understanding of angles
indicator_description: Measure given angles with a protractor and classify them according to their measured sizes – right, acute, obtuse and reflex
examplars:
1. Practice using a protractor.
2. Read and place protractors on worksheet angles.
3. Measure and sort angles into categories.
4. Draw specific numeric angles.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.4.1 Describe the position of objects in space using the cardinal points
indicator_description: Tell the position and motion of objects in space using the cardinal points north, south, east and west
examplars:
1. Describe motion using cardinal points.
2. Describe grid positions from a given location.
3. Provide multi-step directions between grid locations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.3.4.2 Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection) on a 2D shape
indicator_description: Identify images of a single transformation (i.e. reflection) of a 2D shapes in a plane
examplars:
1. Identify reflections across mirror lines in a plane.
2. Categorize objects and images across specific mirror lines.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organisation, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B5.4.1.1 Differentiate between first-hand and second hand data
indicator_description: Explain the difference between first-hand and second-hand data.
examplars:
1. Define first-hand as self-collected vs second-hand as collected by others.
2. Categorize weather data results for different classes.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organisation, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B5.4.1.1 Differentiate between first-hand and second hand data
indicator_description: Find examples of second-hand data in print and electronic media
examplars:
1. Locate league tables and price lists online or in newspapers.
2. Interpret league table data and pose questions.
3. Read and interpret period-allocation data for schools.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organisation, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B5.4.1.2 Construct and interpret double bar graphs to draw conclusions
indicator_description: Draw double bar graphs, complete with title, labelled axes, key or legend, to represent data collected
examplars: 1. Draw graphs for rainfall data across two towns.

subject: Mathematics
sub_strand: Data Collection, Organisation, Presentation, Interpretation and Analysis
content_standard: B5.4.1.2 Construct and interpret double bar graphs to draw conclusions
indicator_description: Identify examples of double bar graphs used in a variety of print and electronic media
examplars:
1. Access, read, and interpret graphs from varied media sources.
2. Interpret school enrollment data over multiple years.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.4.2.1 Describe the likelihood of a single outcome occurring using words such as impossible, possible, and certain
indicator_description: Classify the likelihood of a single outcome occurring in a probability experiment as impossible, possible, or certain
examplars: 1. Classify obtaining specific numbers on a die.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.4.2.1 Describe the likelihood of a single outcome occurring using words such as impossible, possible, and certain
indicator_description: Design and conduct an experiment in which the likelihood of a single outcome occurring is impossible, possible (likely or unlikely), certain.
examplars: 1. Use colored bottle tops to demonstrate varied likelihood outcomes.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B5.4.2.1 Describe the likelihood of a single outcome occurring using words such as impossible, possible, and certain
indicator_description: Conduct a given probability experiment a number of times, recording the outcomes, and explaining the results.
examplars:
1. Toss coins and record outcomes to check predictions.
2. Repeat trials and compare frequency of specific results.
